title: The Lost Cat
introKo:
비 오는 저녁, 전봇대에 붙은 전단지 한 장.
골목에서 만난 하얀 고양이 이야기예요.

quiz.q: 골목에서 찾아 준 고양이의 이름은 무엇인가요?

easy.text: It rains in the evening. You walk home. You see a small paper on a pole.
The paper says: "Lost cat. She is white. Her name is Bori. Please call this number."

options.en: Take a photo of the paper.
options.reaction: You take a photo. You think about the white cat in the cold rain.

options.en: Look around the rainy street for her.
options.reaction: You look around the street. You see only rain. But you think about her now.

easy.text: You walk into a narrow street. Then you hear a small sound. "Meow."
A white cat sits under a wet box. She is small and shivering. It is Bori!

options.reaction: "Bori," you say softly. The cat looks up at you. Then she slowly walks to you.
You open your hand and wait. She smells it. Then she comes closer.

easy.text: Bori comes into your arms. You put her under your coat. You go back to the pole and call the number on the paper.
An old woman answers. Her voice shakes. "You have Bori? Oh, thank you! Please wait there!"
The grandmother comes fast. She holds Bori and cries. "My house is near," she says. "Please come for warm tea."

options.en: Thank you. I would love some tea.
options.reaction: The tea is warm and sweet. Bori sleeps by your feet. The grandmother gives you cookies for home.

options.en: Thank you, but I have to go home.
options.reaction: "Then take this," she says. She gives you a small bag of cookies. It is warm in your hands.

easy.text: A week later, the rain stops. There is a rainbow over the town. You walk by her house. Bori sits in the window. You smile and wave.

normal.text: Rain falls on the town in the evening. On your way home, you see a small paper on a wooden pole.
It says: "Lost cat. She is white, and her name is Bori. Please call this number." The writing is small and shaky.

options.en: Take a photo of the paper.
options.reaction: You save the number with a photo and think about the white cat, somewhere out in this cold rain.

options.en: Look around the rainy street for her.
options.reaction: You look up and down the wet street. There is only rain under the lights, but now you cannot stop thinking about her.

normal.text: You turn into a narrow street. Then, from behind some wet boxes, you hear a small sound. "Meow."
A white cat sits under a soaked box, small and shivering. You know that face. It is Bori!

options.reaction: "Bori," you call softly. Her ears move, she looks up at you, and then she walks to you, slowly.
You open your hand and wait quietly. She smells your fingers, then steps closer, one small step at a time.

normal.text: Bori climbs into your arms. You put her inside your coat, walk back to the pole, and call the number on the paper.
An old woman answers. Her voice shakes. "You found Bori? Oh, thank you, thank you! Please wait there. I am coming now!"
The grandmother arrives, almost running. She holds Bori close and cries quietly. "My house is just there," she says. "Please come in for warm tea."

options.en: Thank you. I would love some tea.
options.reaction: The tea warms your cold hands, and Bori falls asleep by your feet. When you stand up to leave, the grandmother hands you a small bag of cookies.

options.en: Thank you, but I have to go home.
options.reaction: "Then take this, at least," she says, and presses a warm bag of cookies into your hands.

normal.text: A week later, the rain stops and a rainbow hangs over the town. When you walk by her house, Bori is sitting in the window. The grandmother waves, and you wave back.

hard.text: Rain drums softly on the quiet town as you walk home through the early evening. On a wooden pole, a small paper catches your eye.
It reads: "Lost cat. She is white, and her name is Bori. If you see her, please call this number." The handwriting is small and shaky, the kind that belongs to someone old.

options.en: Take a photo of the paper.
options.reaction: You take a photo of the number, thinking of a white cat somewhere out in this cold rain.

options.en: Look around the rainy street for her.
options.reaction: You scan the empty street, but there is only rain falling through the lamplight — and now she is all you can think about.

hard.text: You turn into a narrow street between old walls. Then, from behind a pile of wet boxes, comes a small, thin sound. "Meow."
A white cat is crouching under a soaked box, shivering and pressing herself against the wall. You know that face at once. It is Bori.

options.reaction: "Bori," you call gently. Her ears turn toward you, and after a moment she lifts her head, meets your eyes, and pads toward you.
You crouch down and open your hand, waiting without a word. She smells your fingers, then steps closer, one careful paw at a time.

hard.text: Bori climbs into your arms as if she has been waiting for you. You tuck her inside your coat, hurry back to the pole, and dial the number from the paper.
An old woman answers, and her voice trembles. "You found Bori? Oh, thank you, thank you! Please stay right where you are. I am coming now!"

The grandmother arrives almost at a run, holding an umbrella she has forgotten to open. She gathers Bori into her arms and cries quietly. "My house is just there," she says. "Please come in and warm yourself with a cup of tea."

options.en: Thank you. I would love some tea.
options.reaction: The tea is warm and sweet, and Bori falls asleep against your feet. Before you leave, the grandmother packs a bag of homemade cookies and will not hear a single no.

options.en: Thank you, but I have to go home.
options.reaction: "Then at least take these," she insists, pressing a warm bag of homemade cookies into your hands before you can say a word.

hard.text: A week later, the rain clears and a rainbow rises over the town. Whenever you pass her house now, Bori is sitting in the window like a small white guard, and the grandmother waves at you as if you were an old friend.
